description_long: This object is a set of five rectangular cabinet doors, each attached to a simple rectangular frame, with some doors shown in various states of being open or closed. The doors have a paneled design and are rendered in a light beige or off-white color, giving them a neutral, utilitarian appearance. The frames are minimal, with no visible shelves or interior compartments, and the doors appear to be solid with no glass inserts. The arrangement suggests these could be modular cabinet doors, possibly for use in garages, utility rooms, or storage areas in urban environments. The doors are depicted with visible hinges, and some are shown partially ajar, indicating their openable nature. No handles or knobs are visible, and the interiors of the cabinets are empty, with no contents displayed.
description: A set of five light beige paneled cabinet doors in simple rectangular frames, shown open and closed. The design is minimal, with no visible handles or interior shelves.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five cabinet doors, some open and some closed, with clear visibility of the paneled design.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the doors and frames, with one door clearly open and the rest aligned vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view of the cabinet doors, showing the backs and the open/closed states, with no additional features visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness of the doors and the open door extending outward.
